data.title: Phala Network rebrands as phala.com, a leader in privacy-preserving AI infrastructure. Founded in 2019, it launched the world's largest TEE network.
data.description: In San Francisco, Hashforest Technology shared news that Phala Network has changed its name to phala.com, symbolizing its transition from a Web3 project to a global confidential AI infrastructure firm. Established in 2019 by Marvin Tong and Hang Yin, Phala introduced the largest decentralized Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) network globally, initiating privacy-focused computation for blockchain networks. After enrolling in the NVIDIA Inception Program in 2025, Phala expanded its operations.

data.title: Stock market drops: Dow -249 points, Nvidia rises, Palantir reverses trend. Live coverage details.
data.description: On Tuesday, major stock indexes ended the day down, with the Dow losing 249 points. However, Palantir experienced a turnaround. Nvidia extended its winning streak. This live coverage of the stock market today reveals the latest market movements and trends.

data.title: Essex Property Trust to pay dividend of $2.57/share on Oct 15, 2025 to shareholders of record by Sept 30, 2025.
data.description: In San Mateo, California, Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE:ESS) made known that its Board of Directors has approved a standard quarterly cash dividend of $2.57 per common share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on October 15, 2025, to shareholders on record as of September 30, 2025. Essex Property Trust, Inc. is an S&P 500 company and a REIT that engages in acquiring, developing, redeveloping, and managing multifamily residential properties.

data.title: Sweetgreen's CFO Mitch Reback retires, replaced by Jamie McConnell; Reback's last day is Sept 21, 2025, while McConnell starts on Sept 22, 2025. Reback to stay on in advisory role for six months.
data.description: Los Angeles-based Sweetgreen, Inc. (NYSE: SG) has revealed the retirement of Mitch Reback and the hiring of Jamie McConnell as the new Chief Financial Officer. Reback will retire on September 21, 2025, while McConnell will assume the position on September 22, 2025. Following his retirement, Reback will continue to assist the company in an advisory capacity for a six-month transitional period.

data.title: President Trump claims to fire Fed Gov. Lisa Cook, but she disputes. Conflict arises but no resolution given.
data.description: President Donald Trump announced his decision to dismiss Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook on Monday evening. In response, Cook asserted that Trump does not possess the authority to take such action. The news, originally reported by Investor's Business Daily, highlights the clash between the President and the central bank official.
Akazawa passed letter from Ishiba via Lutnick to Trump

data.title: CoinW founder advocates for transparent and community-driven token listings on exchanges, not based on pay-to-play. Fair models involve strategic alignment, free listings, and community involvement.
data.description: The debate over the fairness of token listings on cryptocurrency exchanges continues to be a prominent issue in the industry. CoinW founder Gary emphasized the importance of exchanges moving away from "pay-to-play" practices and towards more transparent, compliant, and community-focused models. Gary pointed out that obtaining a listing on an exchange does not always require payment, as strategic partnerships may lead to free listings if projects align with the exchange's goals. Additionally, community-driven voting systems allow users to have a direct say in the listing process. Gary believes that a fair model should encompass transparency, merit, compliance, and community engagement. His remarks coincide with the ongoing discussion within the industry. Read the complete story on Benzinga.com.
